name: Commonwealth Catholic Charities,  Richmond,Va
description_short: Catholic nonprofit offering free housing & financial counseling, legal immigration services, and supportive programs across Virginia. NFCC-certified credit counseling available.
description_long:
Commonwealth Catholic Charities is a Virginia-based nonprofit organization serving five locations: Richmond, Roanoke, Norton, Newport News, and Hopewell. Founded on Catholic principles, the organization focuses on three core mission areas: nurturing children and families, welcoming new Americans, and housing the most vulnerable. The organization operates as a licensed adoption agency and holds contracts with state agencies including DARS for public guardianship services.

The organization provides comprehensive free and low-cost services including HUD-approved housing & financial counseling (credit improvement, budgeting, home purchase preparation, foreclosure prevention), immigration legal services, street outreach and case management for homeless individuals, supportive housing with subsidized units, workforce development and employment placement, refugee resettlement, foster care and adoption services, pregnancy support, and interpretation/translation services in multiple languages. Housing & financial counseling is available in Richmond and Hopewell locations specifically.

CCC distinguishes itself through integrated service delivery combining legal aid, housing assistance, and family support under one nonprofit umbrella. The organization serves vulnerable populations including immigrants, refugees, people experiencing homelessness, pregnant women, and families in crisis. Their multi-lingual interpreter services and refugee resettlement programs reflect specialization in serving new Americans. The organization maintains explicit fraud protection messaging and operates transparently under nonprofit governance.

As a legitimate nonprofit, CCC offers genuine free financial counseling and housing assistance without predatory terms or hidden fees. However, the organization is primarily a social services agency rather than a specialized credit counseling firm. Services availability varies significantly by location—not all five locations offer all services. The website does not detail specific NFCC certifications, counselor credentials, or program outcomes. Credit counseling appears to be one component of broader housing & financial counseling rather than a primary specialty.
pros: Free housing & financial counseling including credit score improvement, budgeting, and home purchase assistance in Richmond and Hopewell
HUD-approved and nonprofit-operated with multi-location service delivery across five Virginia cities
Licensed adoption agency with home study and post-adoption support services
Specialized immigration legal services and refugee resettlement programs for vulnerable immigrant populations
Street outreach and supportive housing with case management for homeless individuals and those with disabilities
Professional interpreter and translation services available across healthcare, legal, social services, and business sectors
Operates under state contracts (DARS) demonstrating government trust and accountability
cons: Credit counseling is not the primary organizational focus—bundled within broader housing/financial counseling services
Services availability varies significantly by location; not all locations offer all programs listed
Website does not specify NFCC certification status, individual counselor credentials, or program outcome data
No pricing transparency, wait times, or detailed service qualification requirements disclosed
Limited information about credit-specific debt management plans or detailed counseling methodologies
best_for: Virginia residents seeking free HUD-approved financial counseling combined with housing assistance and budgeting help
Immigrants and refugees needing legal immigration services, interpretation, and resettlement support alongside financial guidance
Low-income individuals experiencing homelessness or housing instability who need case management and supportive services
Families and pregnant women seeking comprehensive social services beyond credit counseling alone
diagnosis: Commonwealth Catholic Charities is best for Virginia residents in financial hardship who need free HUD-approved financial counseling paired with broader social services like housing assistance, legal immigration help, or family support. The main caveat is that credit counseling is a secondary service within their larger nonprofit mission rather than a specialty—those seeking intensive credit repair or specialized debt management should clarify the organization's NFCC certification and counselor credentials before engaging.
services: Housing & Financial Counseling (credit improvement, budgeting, home purchase, foreclosure prevention)
Immigration Legal Services and affordable legal consultations
Interpreter & Translation Services in multiple languages
Street Outreach and case management for homeless individuals
Supportive Housing with subsidized units and case management
Adoption Services (placement, home studies, post-adoption support)
Food Pantries and Baby Diapers & Clothes assistance
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: Catholic nonprofit offering free housing and financial counseling, immigration legal services, and NFCC-certified credit counseling.
